# UC-22.60.10 · ADS-B / Mode S Squitter integrity + injection detection - duplicate ICAO addresses, impossible geometry, ghost-target floods at terminal manoeuvring areas

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Each aircraft broadcasts where it is using a system called ADS-B - like a beacon. If someone fakes a beacon for an aircraft that isn't really there, controllers and other aircraft could be confused. We watch for fake beacons so we can warn air-traffic-control to ignore them.*

---

## Description

Continuously processes ADS-B 1090ES audit events from operator-owned or OpenSky-Network ground stations and detects: duplicate ICAO 24-bit address (a target claiming to be an aircraft already broadcasting), impossible geometry (distance covered in 1 minute exceeding plausible airspeed, vertical speed exceeding airframe capability), and ghost-target floods near terminal manoeuvring areas.

## Value

ADS-B injection has been demonstrated repeatedly in academic conferences (DEFCON, BlackHat) and increasingly in the wild. A confirmed ghost-target flood near a busy terminal area can overload TCAS, distract crews on approach, and create dangerous near-miss situations. This UC closes the operator-side detection gap and supports the ANSP / NCA in identifying ground-based interference sources.

## Implementation

(1) Configure operator-owned ADS-B ground stations or subscribe to OpenSky Network ingest pipeline (`opensky:feed`). (2) Populate `do326a_fleet_icao_24bit_lookup` from the operator's tail-number to ICAO 24-bit address map. (3) Schedule UC-22.60.10 every 5 minutes; route `risk_score >= 90` at severity P1, `risk_score 70-89` at severity P2. (4) Build the `ADS-B Integrity` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.60.10` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-326A-s3-2"`. Every row pins (icao_24bit_address, callsign, anomaly_category, duplicate_count, geometry_violation, geo_region, severity, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Inject a synthetic ADS-B target with a duplicate ICAO 24-bit address that is in the operator's fleet at an impossible position. UC-22.60.10 MUST flag within 5 minutes with risk_score >= 90.

### Negative scenario

Track a normal ADS-B return for the operator's fleet over a 1-hour cruise segment. UC-22.60.10 MUST NOT flag.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 and UC-22.60.2 in production.
- ADS-B ingestion configured (operator stations or OpenSky Network).
- KV Store `do326a_fleet_icao_24bit_lookup`.

## Step 1 - Fleet ICAO mapping

```ini
[do326a_fleet_icao_24bit_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.tail_number = string
field.icao_24bit_address = string
field.airframe_type = string
field.in_fleet = string
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.10: ADS-B Integrity]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -10m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, soar
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.10,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-326A-s3-2
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 90`** (owner: OCC + Chief Pilot + CISO, severity P1): Within 5 minutes, the OCC contacts the affected flight crew (if one of our own tails) and the ANSP; within 24 hours, the NCA is notified under Part-IS IS.OR.230.

**`risk_score 70-89`** (owner: SOC + Flight Ops, severity P2): Within 30 minutes, the operator's flight-planning system flags the affected region for awareness.

## Step 4 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Mode S Squitter overlap**: Mode S surveillance returns the same ICAO 24-bit address; differentiate from ADS-B by message type field.
- **Garmin GDL-90**: GA cross-reference for UAT 978 MHz (not used by commercial in EU/most of the world).
- **NOTAM-protected zones**: ADS-B may be deliberately suppressed near military bases; tag NOTAM regions.

## SPL

```spl
index=aero_adsb sourcetype="adsb:audit"
| bucket _time span=1m
| eventstats count AS station_count by icao_24bit_address _time
| stats max(station_count) AS duplicate_count latest(callsign) AS callsign earliest(lat) AS lat_start latest(lat) AS lat_end earliest(lon) AS lon_start latest(lon) AS lon_end latest(altitude_ft) AS altitude_ft earliest(altitude_ft) AS altitude_start latest(geo_region) AS geo_region by icao_24bit_address _time
| eval distance_nm = round(acos(sin(lat_start*pi()/180)*sin(lat_end*pi()/180) + cos(lat_start*pi()/180)*cos(lat_end*pi()/180)*cos((lon_end - lon_start)*pi()/180)) * 3440, 2)
| eval climb_ft_per_min = (altitude_ft - altitude_start)
| eval geometry_violation = case(
    distance_nm > 25, "impossible-distance-1min",
    abs(climb_ft_per_min) > 6000, "unphysical-vertical-speed",
    1=1, "none")
| lookup do326a_fleet_icao_24bit_lookup icao_24bit_address OUTPUT in_fleet
| eval anomaly_category = case(
    duplicate_count > 1, "duplicate-address",
    geometry_violation != "none", geometry_violation,
    1=1, "in-baseline")
| where anomaly_category != "in-baseline"
| eval risk_score = case(
    anomaly_category = "duplicate-address" AND in_fleet = "true", 100,
    anomaly_category = "duplicate-address", 85,
    anomaly_category = "impossible-distance-1min", 90,
    anomaly_category = "unphysical-vertical-speed", 80,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| eval severity = case(risk_score >= 90, "critical", risk_score >= 70, "high", 1=1, "medium")
| sort 0 -risk_score, -_time
| table _time icao_24bit_address callsign anomaly_category duplicate_count distance_nm climb_ft_per_min geometry_violation geo_region in_fleet severity risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Duplicate-address events by region. (Panel 2) Geometry-violation events by airframe category. (Panel 3) Ghost-target heatmap (lat/lon density). (Panel 4) 30-day ADS-B anomaly trend.

## Known False Positives

1. **ANSP test injection**: EUROCONTROL and FAA may publish controlled ADS-B test signals; tag known test transponders and exclude. 2. **Receiver glitch with duplicate**: a single corrupt packet may appear as a duplicate; require duplicate_count >= 3 across multiple receivers before firing. 3. **Brief reception of legitimate other aircraft with same ICAO**: aircraft re-registered between countries may have legacy ICAO entries; refresh the fleet lookup quarterly.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_10_exceptions` with the Chief Pilot + CISO documented approval.

## References

- [RTCA DO-260C - Minimum Operational Performance Standards for 1090 MHz Extended Squitter Automatic Dependent Surveillance - Broadcast (ADS-B)](https://www.rtca.org/standards/list-of-available-documents/)
- [14 CFR 91.225 - ADS-B Out Equipment and Use](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-14/chapter-I/subchapter-F/part-91/subpart-C/section-91.225)
- [EUROCONTROL ADS-B and Mode S Surveillance](https://www.eurocontrol.int/service/automatic-dependent-surveillance-broadcast-ads-b-out)
